title: Extruding, Forming, Pressing, and Compacting Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines, such as glass-forming machines, plodder machines, and tuber machines, to shape and form products such as glassware, food, rubber, soap, brick, tile, clay, wax, tobacco, or cosmetics.

tasks.text: Adjust machine components to regulate speeds, pressures, and temperatures, and amounts, dimensions, and flow of materials or ingredients.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Press control buttons to activate machinery and equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine, measure, and weigh materials or products to verify conformance to standards, using measuring devices such as templates, micrometers, or scales.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor machine operations and observe lights and gauges to detect malfunctions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Clear jams, and remove defective or substandard materials or products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Notify supervisors when extruded filaments fail to meet standards.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record and maintain production data, such as meter readings, and quantities, types, and dimensions of materials produced.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Review work orders, specifications, or instructions to determine materials, ingredients, procedures, components, settings, and adjustments for extruding, forming, pressing, or compacting machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Turn controls to adjust machine functions, such as regulating air pressure, creating vacuums, and adjusting coolant flow.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean dies, arbors, compression chambers, and molds, using swabs, sponges, or air hoses.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Synchronize speeds of sections of machines when producing products involving several steps or processes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Move materials, supplies, components, and finished products between storage and work areas, using work aids such as racks, hoists, and handtrucks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Activate machines to shape or form products, such as candy bars, light bulbs, balloons, or insulation panels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select and install machine components, such as dies, molds, and cutters, according to specifications, using hand tools and measuring devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Send product samples to laboratories for analysis.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Couple air and gas lines to machines to maintain plasticity of material and to regulate solidification of final products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Pour, scoop, or dump specified ingredients, metal assemblies, or mixtures into sections of machine prior to starting machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure, mix, cut, shape, soften, and join materials and ingredients, such as powder, cornmeal, or rubber to prepare them for machine processing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove materials or products from molds or from extruding, forming, pressing, or compacting machines, and stack or store them for additional processing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Feed products into machines by hand or conveyor.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure arbors and dies to verify sizes specified on work tickets.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Complete work tickets, and place them with products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Disassemble equipment to repair it or to replace parts, such as nozzles, punches, and filters.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove molds, mold components, and feeder tubes from machinery after production is complete.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Swab molds with solutions to prevent products from sticking.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install, align, and adjust neck rings, press plungers, and feeder tubes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
